source_limitations: Dependency profiles are dispersed, reducing robustness of structural variation signals.
No duplication verification was performed to check for exact data duplication artifacts.
Redundancy among backward packet length features limits independent discriminative power.

findings: Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) exhibit stable and significant class-conditioned distribution shifts with high class separation across the examined time windows and network conditions.
Bwd Packet Length Max exhibits low cardinality and near-constant values across classes, indicating limited variability.
Bwd Packet Length Std also shows low cardinality, suggesting constrained variation in backward packet length dispersion.
Bwd Packet Length Mean demonstrates high class separation in distribution, confirming structural differences between BENIGN and DDoS classes.
Overall, backward packet length features show significant class-conditioned structural variation with differing variance ratios and unique value counts.
Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) exhibit moderate shortcut-like signals indicating class-conditioned structural variation.
These features show high variance ratios and differing unique value counts between BENIGN and DDoS classes, supporting the presence of dataset-specific artifacts.
The moderate shortcut signals suggest a risk of overfitting due to representation-sensitive patterns in backward packet length features.
limitations: No duplication verification was performed to check for exact data duplication artifacts.

findings: Backward packet length features exhibit significant class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and differing unique value counts between classes.
High redundancy exists between 'Bwd Packet Length Max', 'Mean', and 'Std', indicating overlapping information rather than independent discriminative signals.
Dependency contextualization did not reveal narrow, robust dependency clusters for 'Bwd Packet Length Max', suggesting limited robustness in structural variation patterns.
limitations: Redundancy among backward packet length features limits independent discriminative power.
Dependency profiles are dispersed, reducing robustness of structural variation signals.
